title: CAC Authentication Monitoring
spl:
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4768,4769,4776)
| search Certificate_Information="*"
| eval fail=if(match(Status,"0x[1-9a-f]"),1,0)
| timechart span=15m sum(fail) as card_failures, count as attempts
| where card_failures/attempts > 0.1
description: Tracks smart card logon failures, PIN lockouts, and certificate issues for DOD/IC access.
value: Tracks smart card logon failures, PIN lockouts, and certificate issues for DOD/IC access.
implementation: Correlate with HSM and CRL availability; help desk runbooks for YubiKey/CAC readers.
visualization: Line chart (failure rate), Table (users with lockouts), Timeline (PKI events).
knownFalsePositives: Smart-card and CAC noise from workstation imaging, PIV reissuance, and visiting contractor machines; use physical access and HSPD-12 records.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Windows Security, PKI.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"` EventCode=4768,4769.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Correlate with HSM and CRL availability; help desk runbooks for YubiKey/CAC readers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4768,4769,4776)
| search Certificate_Information="*"
| eval fail=if(match(Status,"0x[1-9a-f]"),1,0)
| timechart span=15m sum(fail) as card_failures, count as attempts
| where card_failures/attempts > 0.1
```

Understanding this SPL

**CAC Authentication Monitoring** — Tracks smart card logon failures, PIN lockouts, and certificate issues for DOD/IC access.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"` EventCode=4768,4769. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Windows Security, PKI. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ad; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ad, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where card_failures/attempts > 0.1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (failure rate), Table (users with lockouts), Timeline (PKI events).
grandmaExplanation: In our line of business, we pay close attention to "CAC Authentication Monitoring" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
